title: Compression Bandage
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Envenomation.

#### Procedure

Figure 1

Figure 2

Figure 3

- Inform the patient to move as little as possible.

- If possible, mark the location of the envenomation (see **Figure 1**).

- Utilise 2 separate bandages, a roller or crepe (preferred) bandage: 
- Bandage 1 = over the top of the site (see **Figure 2**).
- Bandage 2 = start at the distal end of the limb working your way up proximally (see **Figure 3**).

- Splint limb utilising cardboard splint or anatomical splinting if nil cardboard splint available.

- Confirm distal pulse is present, sensation, and capillary refill.

#### Contraindications

- Envenomation by Red Back spider – consider ice pack.

#### Specific Notes

- If safe to do so, try to identify the creature (e.g. photo, description).
